While traditional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are the standard for image analysis, the self-attention mechanism inherent in transformers offers a promising alternative for capturing complex patterns in images. This research aims to bridge this gap by exploring the effectiveness of vision transformers (ViTs) in the context of chest X-ray interpretation. The study proposes a novel framework that combines the global context awareness of transformers with the local feature extraction capabilities of CNNs. This hybrid approach is tested on the publicly available ChestX-ray14 dataset, benchmarked against traditional CNN models and more recent transformer-based models. The performance is evaluated using standard metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score, with a focus on the detection of subtle pathologies like early-stage lung conditions or minor bone fractures. The results indicate that the hybrid model not only matches the performance of existing models but also demonstrates superior performance in detecting certain fine-grained abnormalities. This study contributes to the growing field of medical AI by demonstrating the potential of vision transformers in diagnostic imaging and opens new avenues for research on transformer-based models in medical contexts
